Improve literature grounding and confidence for Q-019.

Generate discriminative evidence for MECH-094 (hypothesis-tag-as-categorical-write-gate). Past work has 7 PASSing diagnostic probes that confirm the tag exists and routes correctly, plus 1 non_contributory FAIL on the gate-pair test (V3-EXQ-140); zero entries currently feed claim confidence. This is a phase-2 priority because the tag is structurally load-bearing for SD-011 dual-stream gating and the canonical MECH-094 -> confabulation/PTSD mechanism in feedback_psychosis_confabulation_distinction.

Substrate-readiness probe for SD-017 (sleep_phase.minimal_sleep_infrastructure_v3) BEFORE queueing another discriminative pair. SD-017 has 9 prior FAIL runs all classified non_contributory (V3-EXQ-242, 418, 418a, 436, 418d) -- the substrate cannot generate the test conditions a sleep-phase ablation needs. Per feedback_nonstandard_directions.md, the right move for repeated non_contributory is /diagnose-errors then a substrate-prerequisite probe, not yet another ablation that will hit the same wall.

Evidence-grade discriminative pair for SD-017 (sleep_phase.minimal_sleep_infrastructure_v3) -- the follow-up that EXP-0171 / V3-EXQ-500 anticipated. The substrate-readiness probe PASSed but its experiment_purpose=diagnostic excludes it from claim scoring under Option E, so SD-017 still sat at exp_conf=0.000 / quadrant=plausible_unproven despite the substrate being verified-ready. EXP-0171a closes the gap with the same forced-injection template that produced clean discriminative signal for V3-EXQ-499 (MECH-094), V3-EXQ-501 (SD-035), and V3-EXQ-502 (MECH-062).

Generate discriminative evidence for SD-035 (amygdala.analog_substrate). Past work has 4 PASSing diagnostic probes (V3-EXQ-473 cea_mode_prior, V3-EXQ-474 bla_encoding_remap, both x 2 seeds) that confirm BLA encoding remap and CeA mode prior fire as designed; zero entries currently feed claim confidence. The diagnostic probes show the substrate exists; this proposal tests whether the analog form of the substrate is necessary or whether a quantized alternative would suffice.

Generate first experimental evidence for MECH-062 (commitment.tri_loop_gate_coordination). Zero prior experimental entries -- truly fresh-start case. Tests whether the commitment-boundary token must be gate-coordinated across all three BG-like loops (sensorimotor, associative, limbic) or whether a single loop suffices.

Test whether V3's current or enriched environments produce enough world-state information for downstream attribution, SD-016 cue retrieval, and sleep/self-model aggregation to operate. This is an environment-readiness gate, not direct claim-confidence evidence. It follows V3-EXQ-418f/g/h, which showed SD-016 substrate mechanics can work while z_world remains too near-constant to drive behaviour.
Test whether committed operating mode produces measurably lower harm outcomes than ablated (uncommitted) mode, and whether this gap narrows in volatile environments (gate-ablation design).
Ground ARC-029 in habitual vs goal-directed neuroscience: dorsolateral vs dorsomedial striatum dissociation, beta persistence during habitual sequences, model-free/model-based dual-process accounts.
Ground ARC-029 in hierarchical RL and options frameworks: option-critic architecture, Feudal Networks, two-step task model-based switching.

Ground MECH-072 (foreseeable-harm gating reduces false attribution) in causal attribution neuroscience: mirror neuron agency detection, Bayesian causal inference, TPJ role in intentional attribution, cerebellar efference copy in self/other discrimination.
Ground MECH-072 in ML causal attribution and world model separation: Dreamer-style world model, causal representation learning, curiosity-driven exploration.
Validate Route-2 MECH-104 wiring: unexpected harm events should spike commitment uncertainty (beta variance) via surprise gate, distinct from the jitter noise floor baseline.

Deepen biological grounding of MECH-104 (LC-NE volatility interrupt): Yu & Dayan 2005 expected/unexpected uncertainty distinction, Aston-Jones & Cohen adaptive gain theory, Sara norepinephrine and memory consolidation, Bouret & Sara network reset hypothesis.

Ground MECH-104 in computational uncertainty and change-point detection: Nassar surprise-gated learning, Behrens volatility inference, Mathys hierarchical Gaussian filter, Adams & MacKay Bayesian change-point.

V3 core stack integration test: verify SD-008 + SD-007 + SD-003 compose correctly in a single pipeline run now that all three are validated individually. Test that alpha_world=0.9 + reafference correction (z_world_raw_prev input) + counterfactual attribution pipeline together produce expected event selectivity, harm calibration, and attribution accuracy.

Validate that E1 LSTM goal-conditioning (MECH-128) actually shapes E1's predictive distribution. PASS: E1 prediction error is significantly lower for goal-relevant state transitions than goal-irrelevant ones when z_goal_latent is provided vs withheld (interaction term), demonstrating that goal context modulates prediction rather than being a dormant input.

Reconfirm the post-SD-011 SD-003 attribution result in its current form: can the trained z_harm_s counterfactual attribution path cleanly beat an explicit ablation or scramble control across matched seeds?
Test whether the dual nociceptive stream substrate is stable enough to be treated as platform rather than rescue work.
Resolve the representation-vs-selection ambiguity by injecting a handcrafted resource indicator into the goal path: if the selector gets an explicit goal cue, can it exploit it behaviorally?
Show genuine goal-directed behavior with the current best learned goal signal: does GOAL_PRESENT beat GOAL_ABSENT under matched conditions?
Demonstrate the selector payoff directly: does a shared harm-plus-goal selector outperform a one-sided selector in a hazard-bearing goal task?

Ground MECH-128 (E1 goal-context conditioning) in predictive coding and top-down goal modulation literature: Rao & Ballard 1999 (predictive coding, top-down context), Clark 2013 (predictive brains with goal-directed top-down), Friston 2010 (free-energy, goal context in generative models), Botvinick & Plaut 2004 (goal-conditioned recurrent networks for action sequences).

Ground MECH-129 (relational harm: harm-to-agent vs harm-to-agency) in philosophy of harm and AI alignment literature. Hart & Honore on causal obstruction, Thomson on trolley problem harm distinctions, Shue on agency as a protectable interest, Pearlman on goal obstruction as instrumental harm, Hadfield-Menell on other-agent goal inference.

Ground MECH-130 (curiosity typing: world-state novelty vs agent-policy novelty) in intrinsic motivation and MARL exploration literature. Schmidhuber compression-based novelty, Oudeyer & Kaplan typology, Pathak ICM, Houthooft VIME, Baker multiagent autocurricula. Key note: absence of world vs agent novelty distinction in the literature may itself be a contribution.

Replace hardcoded commitment_threshold=0.40 with k*warmup_baseline_variance (k=2) per EXQ-018b findings. Requires: (1) measuring baseline_variance during warmup episodes, (2) setting commitment_threshold = 2 * baseline_variance after warmup completes. Converts ARC-016 from manually-calibrated to self-calibrating as operating regime changes.

Characterize the hazard/resource ratio threshold above which the depression attractor forms (behavioral flattening: HABIT==PLANNED). EXQ-237a established the attractor exists at LONG_HORIZON parameters; this sweep identifies boundary conditions.

Systematically replicate and characterize the computational depression attractor from EXQ-237a seed=42. Establish that z_harm_a elevation + z_goal failure -> HABIT/PLANNED flattening is robust across seeds.

Test the phase-transition recovery prediction: once the depression attractor is established (LONG_HORIZON), switching to LOW_HARM environment produces non-linear delayed recovery, not immediate improvement.
Test whether artificially maintaining a minimum VALENCE_WANTING floor (simulating serotonergic benefit terrain maintenance) prevents depression attractor formation in LONG_HORIZON condition.

Test bidirectional gain effects on z_goal seeding: reduced gain replicates depression in SIMPLE/LOW_HARM condition; elevated gain rescues LONG_HORIZON from depression attractor. Bidirectional prediction is a strong discriminating test.
Test whether injecting a sustained z_goal signal (simulating PFC top-down goal persistence) prevents behavioral flattening during LONG_HORIZON even when benefit terrain has collapsed.

Test whether any single serotonergic rescue mechanism (MECH-186, 187, or 188 in isolation) fully restores LONG_HORIZON performance to SIMPLE baseline, or whether full recovery requires all three. Confirms INV-052: tonic regulatory system is necessary across all three pipeline stages.

Ground INV-053 (computational depression attractor) in computational psychiatry, distinguishing motivational terrain collapse from aberrant reward learning. Huys/Dayan/Adams lineage focuses on incorrect Q-values and precision weighting; INV-053 is about the substrate (benefit terrain, z_goal seeding) degrading. Establish this as a distinct failure mode.

Ground MECH-187 (incentive salience gain regulation by serotonin) in 5-HT neuromodulation and incentive salience literature. Serotonin modulates dopaminergic incentive salience attribution; MECH-187 formalizes this as gain on z_goal seeding from benefit terrain signals.

Ground INV-054 (depressive maintenance loop as self-sustaining attractor; recovery as phase transition) in clinical depression phenomenology and treatment response literature. Phase-transition prediction (non-linear, delayed recovery) is a unique testable consequence of the INV-054 maintenance loop.

Re-run EXQ-228a theta bypass behavioral test after EXQ-247 resolves z_goal seeding. Protocol unchanged -- the experiment design is sound, only the substrate precondition (z_goal_norm >= 0.05) was unmet.
Test Go/NoGo sub-channel symmetry with real z_goal. Requires both z_goal_norm >= 0.1 AND z_goal alignment with resource proximity (align_corr > 0.2). Two gates.

Redesigned dual-system discrimination test. Must include: (a) repeated identical environments where monostrategy IS the correct behavior (habit detection phase), then (b) novel environments where planned pathway should add value over habit.
Redesigned replay diversity test. Must pair replay mechanism with explicit exploration to provide diverse source material. Test asks: does balanced replay MAINTAIN diversity that exploration created?
Redesigned sleep phase ablation with context-conditioned action selection. EXQ-242 showed slot differentiation works (4/5 seeds) but agent doesn't use context knowledge. Add context->action pathway.

First V3 test of SD-029 (single-pass z_harm_s comparator). Replace mean-cf-gap with event-conditioned residual analysis on approach-to-harm events; include event-density controls (guaranteed minimum agent-caused-hazard events per seed) to fix the EXQ-431 sample-starvation failure mode.

Test MECH-257 dual-function claim: does a single E2_harm_s substrate support both retrospective comparator (SD-029 attribution) and prospective rollout-scoring (evaluator) effectively, or does joint training degrade one mode relative to mode-split baselines?

Falsify (or support) the MECH-261 claim that a shared operating-mode vector from SD-032a is load-bearing for forward-propagation of internal_replay content into subsequent external_task action selection. Core hypothesis: lesioning the coordinator abolishes the post-quiescence action-selection bias; local per-substrate gating alone does not reproduce it.

Test whether MECH-261's per-subdivision write-landing profile is correctly mode-routed -- distinct from the coordinator-level test in EXP-0148. EXP-0148 asks whether the coordinator overlay produces the right aggregate forward-propagation bias; EXP-0149 asks whether writes actually land in the correct PFC subdivision (SD-033a vs SD-033b) under each mode. A coordinator could pass EXP-0148 by producing the right aggregate bias while writes are mis-routed internally between subdivisions; only a per-subdivision lesion design can dissociate that.

Probe whether a committed rule_state whose completion condition is satisfied is actually released: beta drops, action-class No-Go is installed, residue tagged to that rule is discharged. This is the ocd4 verified-but-not-released row -- OCD's canonical loop-persistence failure.

Probe whether rule_state / goal field persists across a delay window where external reward is deferred and intervening distractors are present. Tests Hold robustness -- the OCD over-binding axis predicts excessive persistence, the depression under-binding axis predicts premature collapse.

Probe whether a rule installed during internal_planning mode survives the transition to external_task via the SD-033a rule_state substrate, and whether it does so without relying on hippocampal re-proposal. If MECH-267 mode-conditioned hippocampal proposals ARE the carrier, the rule/hippocampus role allocation is wrong.

Probe whether dACC prediction-error signal caps and habituates under repeated identical outcomes. Without saturation, a stuck governance loop generates unbounded pe, which is the rumination signature identified in the 2026-04-20 GAP MEMO.

Probe whether SD-032a asymmetric mode hysteresis (Schmitt trigger: enter_threshold != exit_threshold) is necessary for reproducing task-switching cost asymmetries, or whether the existing MECH-259 symmetric threshold + lateral inhibition in Go selection suffices.

Probe whether hypothesis-tagged simulation content (MECH-094) is filtered out of action-selection Go pathway under OCD-like intrusion pressure. Specifically: does mode-conditioned hippocampal proposal (MECH-267) prevent replay content from bypassing MECH-094 into policy writes?

Probe whether a sufficient-but-not-optimal action produces correct residue discharge and No-Go refractory via SD-034 closure. The ocd4 satisficing row: OCD fails to accept sufficient action, demanding exact-match completion -- unreachable.

Probe whether varying exit_threshold within a single mode reproduces the clinical under-binding / over-binding phenotype continuum on behavioural markers. Companion test to EXP-0160; EXP-0160 tests the existence of asymmetry, EXP-0163 tests dose-response along the axis.

Probe the interaction between SD-034 closure on successful completion and MECH-268 pe saturation on sustained counter-evidence. The ocd4 commitment-vs-contradiction row: how does the system adjudicate between 'completion achieved, release' and 'counter-evidence mounting, release via different route'?

Probe whether SD-033e FrontopolarAnalog's MECH-264 counterfactual-value head tracks the value of the best-unchosen alternative over committed trajectories, as distinct from the SD-033c dlPFC working-memory substrate. Boorman 2009 Neuron finding: lateral FPC tracks chosen-vs-unchosen option value across trials, not working-memory contents. Targets falsification signature (i) in SD-033e spec: 'counterfactual-value tracking over K>=2 alternatives'.

Probe whether FPC ablation (zeroing the SD-033e module) reproduces the Mansouri 2015 (PNAS) bilateral-FPC-lesion phenotype: over-focus on one goal plus impaired adaptation to a novel rule. This is the falsification signature (iv) in SD-033e spec: 'disengagement for exploration'. Distinguishes SD-033e contribution from SD-033a (rule) and dACC (value integration).

Dissociate SD-033c (dlPFC working-memory) from SD-033e (frontopolar counterfactual / relative-importance) behaviourally. Burgess 2007 (TiCS) gateway hypothesis predicts medial BA 10 external engagement vs lateral BA 10 internal cognition; the two substrates should be selectively ablatable on different task demands.

Test the MECH-285 Q4 coupling (SYNTHESIS 2026-04-24 verdict 2): sleep-phase replay priority should scale proportionally (not threshold-gated) with integrated waking staleness. A waking session with high trigger-event load on region R should produce higher sleep replay event count over R than a matched low-load session, with effect size approximately monotonic in the difference in staleness[R] at sleep-mode entry.

Test OCD Layer 2 hypothesis from REE_assembly/docs/architecture/psychiatric_failure_modes.md (2026-04-28 OCD section): does the MECH-290 backward credit sweep -- which reinforces residue terrain along committed trajectories via VALENCE_WANTING writes scaled by gamma decay from outcome quality -- contribute to V3 monostrategy by self-reinforcing the planner's preferred trajectory shape? PASS = ablating MECH-290 attenuates baseline monostrategy; supports Layer 2 (chunk-side runaway analogue) as a contributor and licenses SD-045 action_chunk_cache pull-forward to V3 to add an explicit chunk No-Go (the architectural fix). FAIL = MECH-290 ablation has no monostrategy effect; rules out Layer 2, escalates to Layer 3 (SD-046 multi-slot GoalState pull-forward) as the next architectural diagnostic.

Exploratory probe of cross-substrate threshold coherence under sustained chronic load. Test whether the existing scattered adaptive loci (ARC-016, SD-032c, SD-032d, SD-032e) move along a shared low-dimensional trajectory under sustained drive_level=0.9 -- consistent with an implicit joint signal, licensing Q-041 closure as 'no separate supervisor needed' -- or move independently, licensing promotion to a SD cluster (supervisor + sleep-writeback MECH + volatility-tracker MECH). This is a research-anchor probe, not a make-or-break test; the goal is to characterise the regime, not to gate a single claim.

Tune REEConfig.rem_precision_recalibration_step (Option A linear interpolation gain on E3._running_variance toward 1.0/persistent_zero_point) by sweeping it across a principled range and identifying the regime where post-REM precision tracks the captured zero-point reference without over-correcting or under-correcting. Plan-of-record Q1 in REE_assembly/evidence/planning/sleep_substrate_plan.md proposed default 0.1 as a placeholder; this proposal closes the open question with empirical evidence rather than guesswork. V3-EXQ-541a confirmed F1 substrate works at step=0.1 (C1+C2 PASS) but C3 cross-arm divergence stayed under threshold; this sweep tests whether step tuning alone clears C3 or whether Phase 7 / Option B becomes load-bearing.

MECH-320 substrate-readiness diagnostic: verify the additive vigor-bias regulator instantiates correctly, master-OFF preserves baseline behaviour bit-identically, lift-arithmetic sweep produces monotone shift in action-vs-no-op score gap, and the EWMA scalar updates correctly in response to an injected reward stream. UC1 instantiation, UC2 master-OFF backward-compat, UC3 lift-arithmetic sweep, UC4 select_action wiring contract via act_with_split_obs, UC5 simulation-gate harmless (vigor scalar should not write through MECH-094 hypothesis-tag gate during E2 rollouts).

note: Lightweight surfacing index -- no acceptance_checks or pack_contract. For full proposal detail use experiment_proposals.v1.json via bash.
